name: Stir-Fried Beef and Vegetables
recipeIngredient: 8 ounces beef (you can use almost any cut - rump steak or flank steak is good) -- marinated
4 ounces green pepper or 4 ounces broccoli or 4 ounces mung bean sprouts
1 teaspoon wine (Chinese rice wine or pale dry sherry)
1 teaspoon cornstarch mixed with 1 tablespoon cold water or chicken stock
recipeInstructions:
1. Marinate the beef in a standard marinade for at least 15 minutes.
2. Heat the wok and add enough peanut oil to stir-fry the vegetables.
3. Add the green vegetable first and stir-fry it for 10-20 seconds before adding the onion, as you like your onions crispy and crunchy, which takes less time to cook than bell peppers or broccoli or bean sprouts.
4. Stir-fry until three-quarters done. Remove the vegetables and set aside, keeping them warm.
5. Wipe out the wok and reheat it until very hot. Add about 1-2 tablespoons of peanut oil.
6. Add garlic (and ginger if desired) and stir-fry for about 10 seconds.
7. Add the meat and stir-fry for about 60 seconds.
8. Put the vegetables back in, add the soy sauce and wine.
9. Stir the cornstarch mixture to recombine it and then add.
10. Continue to lift, toss, stir, etc., until the sauce is thickened and translucent.
11. Just before serving, add a few splashes of sesame oil. Serve with plain rice.
